title.en: Polite Returns and Exchanges: "It's a bit different from what I expected."
title.ko: 생각했던 거랑 조금 달라서요 (정중하게 환불/교환 요청하기)

description.en: Need to return or exchange something? Learn the polite way to state your reason using soft endings like "작아서요" to get a smooth refund.
description.ko: 물건을 샀는데 마음이 바뀌었을 때! 상대를 탓하지 않고 정중하게 이유를 설명하며 환불이나 교환을 이끌어내는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Shopping. Refund. Exchange. Manners. Reason. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 쇼핑백을 들고 망설이는 목소리로 바로 시작
content.text: Mina, I bought this shirt yesterday, but I need to return it. It just doesn't work for me.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I’m worried the staff might think I’m being a "difficult customer." How can I ask for a refund without sounding like I'm complaining?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 내 상황을 정중하게 설명하는 게 중요해요.
content.translation: Ah, in that case, it's important to politely explain your situation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 한국에서는..., "바꿔주세요" 라고 바로 요구하기보다는 "저한테 좀 작아서요" 또는..., "색상이 생각했던 거랑 달라서요" 라고 이유를 먼저 말해요.
content.translation: In Korea, instead of demanding "Please change it" right away, we give a reason first, like "It's a bit small for me" or "The color is different from what I thought."

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh, so I provide the reason first so the conversation flows more naturally?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 오늘은 상대방이 기분 나쁘지 않게 내 의사를 전달하는 환불과 교환의 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Exactly! Today, we'll learn refund and exchange reactions that convey your intentions without making the other person feel bad!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 생각했던 거랑 조금 달라서요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Using this phrase makes it sound like a personal preference issue, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정확해요! 제품 탓을 하는 게 아니라..., "내 생각과 다르다" 라고 말하니까 훨씬 부드럽죠.
content.translation: Exactly! It's much softer because you're saying "it's different from my thought" rather than blaming the product.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to give a more specific reason, like the size?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "사이즈가 안 맞아서요" 라고 말해보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying "Because the size doesn't fit."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "작아서요" 혹은..., "커서요" 라고 구체적으로 말하면 더 정확한 서비스를 받을 수 있어요.
content.translation: If you say specifically "Because it's small" or "Because it's big," you can get more accurate service.

insight.title: 사이즈가 안 맞아서요
insight.usage_tip: 사이즈(Size) + 가(Subject marker) + 안 맞다(Not fit) + 아서요(Because/Polite ending).

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm at the service desk. I've decided to refund the shirt because it's small AND the color is off.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저기요 실례합니다. 어제 산 옷을 환불하고 싶어서요.
content.translation: Excuse me. I'd like to get a refund for the clothes I bought yesterday.

insight.title: 환불하고 싶어서요
insight.usage_tip: Ending with '~어서요' makes your intention sound very soft and polite.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 무슨 문제가 있을까요?
content.translation: Ah, yes! Is there a problem?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 옷은 정말 예쁜데 저한테 좀 작아서요.
content.translation: Ah, the clothes are really pretty, but they are a bit small for me.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그리고 집에 가서 보니까 색상도 생각했던 거랑 조금 달라서요.
content.translation: And seeing it at home, the color is also a bit different from what I expected.

insight.title: 생각했던 거랑 조금 달라서요
insight.usage_tip: John is logically listing why the item doesn't work for him, which makes the refund request very clear.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그러시군요. 사이즈도 작고 색상도 마음에 안 드시면 바로 환불 도와드릴게요.
content.translation: Ah, I see. Since the size is small and you're not happy with the color, I'll help you with the refund right away.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 네, 감사합니다!
content.translation: Yes, thank you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Phew! Listing the reasons clearly like that made the whole process much more logical.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "작아서요" "달라서요" 처럼 정중하게 이유를 대는 리액션이 성공적인 환불의 비결이랍니다.
content.translation: Right. Reacting with polite reasons like "Because it's small" or "Because it's different" is the secret to a successful refund.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "shopping correction" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 생각했던 거랑 조금 달라서요!
content.translation: It's a bit different from what I expected!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 사이즈가 안 맞아서요!
content.translation: Because the size doesn't fit!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저한테 좀 작아서요!
content.translation: Because it's a bit small for me!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 환불할 수 있을까요?
content.translation: Could I get a refund?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 영수증 여기 있습니다!
content.translation: Here is the receipt!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 쇼핑 후에 고민이 된다면, 당황하지 말고 정중하게 이유를 말해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if you're concerned after shopping, don't panic and state your reason politely.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "생각했던 거랑 조금 달라서요!"
content.translation: It's a bit different from what I thought!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. We've finished Part 2 of Season 2!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 우리 다음 파트에서 더 실무적인 상황으로 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: Let's meet with more practical situations in the next part. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 영수증이 없으면 환불이 어려울 수 있어요. (Without a receipt, a refund might be difficult.)
사이즈가 작으면 더 큰 것으로 교환하세요. (If the size is small, exchange it for a larger one.)
이 디자인은 색상이 다양해서 고르기 좋아요. (This design has various colors, so it's good to choose from.)
물건을 산 후에는 영수증을 꼭 챙기세요. (Make sure to take the receipt after buying something.)
정당한 사유가 있으면 언제든 교환이 가능해요. (If there is a valid reason, an exchange is possible anytime.)

vocabulary.meaning: To get angry / To blush with anger
vocabulary.example: 사소한 일로 사람들과 얼굴 붉히고 싶지 않아요. (I don't want to get angry with people over trivial matters.)
